title: agrostemma involucratum
intro: Agrostemma involucratum, also known as Corn Cockle, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its pink or white flowers and distinctive seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Agrostemma involucratum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments from respiratory issues to skin conditions. However, due to the potential toxicity of the plant's seeds, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any Agrostemma involucratum products.
